title: cyclophyllum maritimum
intro: Cyclophyllum maritimum, also known as the sea garcinia, is a tropical plant species native to the Pacific Islands, characterized by its small white flowers and edible fruits. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cyclophyllum maritimum include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, making it a valuable herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Cyclophyllum maritimum typically involve the use of its leaves, fruits, or roots, which can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
